name: La Ralla & El Rallon – Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales
name_original: La Ralla & El Rallón – Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales
region_intro: Hiking in Bardenas Reales offers exploration of a semi-desert natural region in southeast Navarra, Spain. This UNESCO Biosphere Reserve is characterized by its unique badlands topography, featuring ravines, plateaus, and striking rock formations. The landscape is sculpted from clay, chalk, and sandstone, creating a visually distinct environment often described as "lunar." This area provides a variety of trails through its extensive plains and deep gorges. Best hiking trails in Bardenas Reales The most popular hiking route is Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales – circular to Cabezo de Piskerra, a 3.4 miles (5.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 33 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the iconic Piskerra formation. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña del Fraile Trail, an easy 3.0 miles (4.9 km) path. This trail winds through the characteristic semi-desert terrain, showcasing the region's unique geological features. Local hikers also love the La Ralla & El Rallon – Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail leading through the Bardena Blanca area, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Hiking in Bardenas Reales is defined by its badlands topography, striking clay and sandstone formations, and extensive plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Bardenas Reales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Bardenas Reales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscape can I expect when hiking in Bardenas Reales?
A: Bardenas Reales is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve known for its unique semi-desert, 'lunar' landscape. You'll encounter badlands topography, featuring ravines, plateaus, and striking rock formations sculpted by wind and water erosion from clay, chalk, and sandstone. It's a visually captivating environment, often described as moon-like.

Q: Are there different difficulty levels for hiking trails in Bardenas Reales?
A: Yes, Bardenas Reales offers a variety of trails suitable for different fitness levels. You can find easy walks, moderate hikes, and more challenging routes. For instance, there are over 20 easy routes, 28 moderate routes, and 10 difficult routes available for exploration.

Q: What are some notable geological formations or landmarks to see?
A: The region is famous for its iconic rock formations. The most famous is &lt;strong&gt;Castildetierra&lt;/strong&gt;, a striking clay pyramid located in the Bardena Blanca area, which is easily accessible. Another impressive formation is &lt;strong&gt;Piskerra&lt;/strong&gt;, offering panoramic views. You can explore these areas on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970756452/parque-natural-de-las-bardenas-reales-circular-to-cabezo-de-piskerra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales – circular to Cabezo de Piskerra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bardenas Reales?
A: The best time for hiking in Bardenas Reales is between September and June. During these months, temperatures are more pleasant for outdoor activities. It's advisable to avoid the extreme heat of summer and the potentially muddy conditions that can occur after heavy rain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Bardenas Reales?
A: Yes, Bardenas Reales has several easy trails suitable for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970756452/parque-natural-de-las-bardenas-reales-circular-to-cabezo-de-piskerra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales – circular to Cabezo de Piskerra&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.4 km route that takes about 1 hour 33 minutes and offers views of the iconic Piskerra formation, making it a great option for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Bardenas Reales?
A: While Bardenas Reales is a natural park, specific regulations regarding dogs can vary. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect the fragile ecosystem and local wildlife. Always check local signage or the park's official guidelines before heading out with your pet.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking?
A: The semi-desert environment of Bardenas Reales is home to unique flora and fauna. Hikers might spot various bird species, including eagles and vultures, as well as foxes and reptiles. Staying on marked paths helps protect these animals and their habitats.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Bardenas Reales?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970756452/parque-natural-de-las-bardenas-reales-circular-to-cabezo-de-piskerra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales – circular to Cabezo de Piskerra&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 5.4 km loop. Another moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10464965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paso de los Ciervos – Bardenas Reales Cliffs loop from La Ralla&lt;/a&gt;, covering about 11.5 km.

Q: How long do hikes typically take in Bardenas Reales?
A: Hiking durations vary significantly depending on the trail's length and difficulty. Easy routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968815959/pena-del-fraile-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Fraile Trail&lt;/a&gt; (4.9 km), can take around 1.5 to 2 hours. Longer, moderate trails such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970772529/la-ralla-el-rallon-parque-natural-de-las-bardenas-reales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ralla & El Rallon&lt;/a&gt; (11.8 km) might take over 3 hours.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Bardenas Reales?
A: The hiking routes in Bardenas Reales are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique badlands topography, striking clay and sandstone formations, and the immersive experience of exploring this semi-desert landscape.

Q: Is public transport available to reach Bardenas Reales for hiking?
A: Public transport options directly into the heart of Bardenas Reales are limited due to its remote, natural park status. Most visitors access the park by car. For detailed information on reaching the region, you may find resources on the official tourism website for Navarra: &lt;a href="https://www.visitnavarra.es/en/bardenas-reales/-/categories/15329196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitnavarra.es&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting Bardenas Reales for hiking?
A: There are designated parking areas at key access points and near popular attractions within Bardenas Reales. For instance, there is parking available close to the famous Castildetierra formation, allowing for a short walk to the landmark. Always follow local signage for official parking spots.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots I shouldn't miss?
A: Absolutely. The region offers several stunning viewpoints. Don't miss the &lt;strong&gt;Bardena Blanca Viewpoint&lt;/strong&gt; for expansive vistas of the central, most eroded area. The &lt;strong&gt;El Rallón Viewpoint&lt;/strong&gt; also provides breathtaking panoramic views of the unique landscape. These spots are ideal for photography and appreciating the scale of the badlands.
tips: This is another of the natural viewpoints of the Bardenas that are well worth a visit. It helps you get an idea of the scale of the huge and magical place you are in.
For the last 15 m you should be sure-footed and free from vertigo. You will be rewarded with a beautiful panoramic view.
